steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Read the y-intercept of data set A's line of best fit from the graph, then apply the vertical shift: subtracting 9 from every y-value moves the entire line down by 9 units, so the new y-intercept is the original minus 9.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $45.57$: The graph's line crosses the y-axis at approximately $54.57$. Subtracting 9 gives $54.57 - 9 = 45.57$. ✓
  • Choice B — $53$: This is close to the original y-intercept, as if no shift was applied. ✗
  • Choice C — $59.62$: This exceeds the original y-intercept — it would require adding rather than subtracting. ✗
  • Choice D — $62.75$: Further above the original intercept; a common error from misreading the graph scale. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
